children.text: Retired pay base for members who first became members before
Use of Retired Pay Base in Computing Retired Pay.—
The retired pay or retainer pay of any person entitled to that pay who first became a member of a uniformed service before

children.tail: , is computed using the retired pay base or retainer pay base determined under this section.

children.text: Recomputation of retired or retainer pay to reflect later active duty is provided for under

children.tail: without reference to a retired pay base or retainer pay base.

children.text: Retirement Under Subtitle A or E.—
Disability, warrant officer, and dopma retirement
In the case of a person whose retired pay is computed under this subtitle, the retired pay base is determined in accordance with the following table.

children.tail: Compute at rates applicable on date of retirement or date when member’s name was placed on temporary disability retired list, as the case may be.
Compute at rates applicable on date of retirement.
For the purposes of this subsection, determine member’s retired grade as if sections 7342 and 9342 did not apply.
of grade to which member is entitled under section 1372 or to which he was entitled on day before retirement or placement on temporary disability retired list, whichever is higher.

In the case of a member whose retired pay is computed under

children.tail: or who is entitled to retired pay computed under
, the retired pay base is determined in accordance with the following table.
For the purposes of this subsection, determine member’s retired grade as if section 7342 did not apply.

children.text: Monthly basic pay of member’s retired grade.
Monthly basic pay to which member was entitled on day before he retired.
Monthly basic pay of grade to which member is advanced on retired list.
Rate of basic pay to be used
The rate of basic pay to be used under paragraph (1) is the rate applicable on the date of the member’s retirement.

children.tail: If the rate specified is less than the pay of any warrant officer grade satisfactorily held by the member on active duty, use the monthly basic pay of that warrant officer grade.

children.text: Basic pay of the grade in which the member retired.
Basic pay of the grade the officer would hold if he had not received an appointment described in section 8325(b).
Basic pay of the pay grade in which the member was serving on the day before retirement.

Rate of basic pay to be used

In the case of a member who is retired under any section of title 14, the member’s retired pay is computed under section 423(a)

of title 14 in the manner provided in that section.

children.text: Commissioned Corps of National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration
In the case of an officer whose retired pay is computed under section 245 of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration Commissioned Officer Corps Act of 2002 (

children.tail: ), the retired pay base is the basic pay of the rank with which the officer retired.

children.text: Commissioned Corps of Public Health Service
In the case of an officer who is retired under section 210(g) or 211(a) of the Public Health Service Act (

children.tail: , 212(a)), the retired pay base is determined as follows:

children.text: If the officer is retired under section 210(g) of such Act, the retired pay base is the basic pay of the permanent grade held by the officer at the time of retirement.

If the officer is retired under section 211(a) of such Act, the retired pay base is the basic pay of the highest grade held by the officer and in which, in the case of a temporary promotion to such grade, the officer has performed active duty for not less than six months.
Special Rule for Former Chairmen and Vice Chairmen of the JCS, Chiefs of Service, Chief of the National Guard Bureau, Commanders of Combatant Commands, and Senior Enlisted Members.—

For the purposes of subsections (b) through (e), in determining the rate of basic pay to apply in the determination of the retired pay base of a member who has served as Chairman or Vice Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, as a Chief of Service, as Chief of the National Guard Bureau, as a commander of a unified or specified combatant command (as defined in

children.tail: ), or as the senior enlisted member of an armed force or the senior enlisted advisor to the Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff or the Chief of the National Guard Bureau, the highest rate of basic pay applicable to the member while serving in that position shall be used, if that rate is higher than the rate otherwise authorized by this section.

children.text: Exception for members reduced in grade or who do not serve satisfactorily
Paragraph (1) does not apply in the case of a member who, while or after serving in a position specified in that paragraph and by reason of conduct occurring after
in the case of an enlisted member, is reduced in grade as the result of a court-martial sentence, nonjudicial punishment, or other administrative process; or
in the case an officer, is not certified by the Secretary of Defense under section 1370(c)

children.tail: of this title as having served on active duty satisfactorily in the grade of general or admiral, as the case may be, while serving in that position.

children.text: The term “Chief of Service” means any of the following:
Chief of Staff of the Army.
Chief of Staff of the Air Force.
The term “senior enlisted member” means any of the following:
Master Chief Petty Officer of the Navy.
Chief Master Sergeant of the Air Force.
Sergeant Major of the Marine Corps.
Chief Master Sergeant of the Space Force.
Master Chief Petty Officer of the Coast Guard.
